loadingScreen.welcome: Welcome to the AI Chat Assistant

themeFont.title: Theme & Font

customization.title: Interface Customization
customization.subtitle: Customize chat interface, message bubbles and toolbar appearance

topToolbar.secondary: Customize components and layout of top toolbar with drag-n-drop

thinkingProcess.secondary: Customize how AI thinking process is displayed and auto-collapse behavior

messageBubble.secondary: Adjust bubble style and width settings

inputBox.secondary: Customize input box style and layout

devTools.title: Developer Tools
devTools.subtitle: Settings for debugging and performance monitor

performanceMonitor.secondary: Display real-time FPS, scroll events, render time and memory usage in chat view

theme.label: Theme
theme.helper: Select the app theme. System will follow your device's light/dark mode.

fontSize.label: Global Font Size
fontSize.helper: Adjust the base font size of all texts in the app, affecting chat messages and interface texts

font.label: Global Font
font.helper: Select a global font for the app, affecting the appearance of all interface texts

interaction.title: Interaction
interaction.subtitle: Customize interaction style and notification settings

sendWithEnter.secondary: Press Enter to send quickly, use Shift+Enter for new line

notifications.secondary: Show system notification when AI reply is complete

web-search.title: Web Search
web-search.desc: Configure web search and related services

knowledge-settings.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge-settings.desc: Manage KB configs and embedding models

list.title: Server List

list.emptyDesc: You can manually add a new server or choose from built-in templates.

status.fillRequired: Please fill in the required fields

quickActions.title: Quick Actions
quickActions.subtitle: Quickly import or add built-in servers
quickActions.importDesc: Bulk import server configurations from a JSON file
quickActions.builtinDesc: Browse and add pre-configured built-in servers

dialog.importDesc: Paste the JSON content that conforms to the format to bulk import servers. Here is an example format:
dialog.builtinDesc: Here are some pre-configured common MCP servers that you can add to your list with one click.

form.name: Server Name

quickPhrase.title: Quick Phrase Management
quickPhrase.subtitle: Manage your quick phrases to quickly insert frequently used content during chats.

displaySettings.title: Display Settings
displaySettings.label: Show quick phrase button in the input box
displaySettings.caption: Control whether to display the quick phrase button in the chat input box

empty.title: No quick phrases yet
empty.subtitle: Create your first quick phrase to make chatting more efficient

empty.title: No workspaces yet
empty.description: Create a workspace to manage your files and documents, helping the AI better understand your projects.

deleteDialog.title: Delete Workspace
deleteDialog.content: Are you sure you want to delete the workspace "{{name}}"?
deleteDialog.warning: This will only delete the workspace configuration, not the actual files on your computer.

providerSection.title: Model Providers
providerSection.subtitle: Configure multiple model providers. Click to manage settings.

topicNaming.secondary: Configure model and options for auto topic naming

selectorStyle.dialog: Current: Dialog (Click to switch to dropdown)
selectorStyle.dropdown: Current: Dropdown (Click to switch to dialog)

modelSection.title: Topic Naming Model
modelSection.subtitle: Select the AI model used to automatically generate titles for topics

enableSwitch.secondary: Automatically generate a suitable title for a new topic when the conversation starts

promptSection.title: Topic Naming Prompt
promptSection.subtitle: Custom prompt for topic naming. Leave blank for default.
promptSection.placeholder: e.g., Generate a concise title of no more than 5 words for the following conversation...

agentPrompts.title: Agent Prompt Library

systemPromptVar.title: System Prompt Variable Injection
systemPromptVar.desc: Automatically inject time, location and other dynamic variables into system prompts
systemPromptVar.info: When enabled, the system will automatically append the corresponding variable information to the end of the system prompt when sending a message.

time.title: Time Variable
time.tip: Time info will be appended to the end of the system prompt

location.title: Location Variable
location.tip: Location info will be appended to the end of the system prompt
location.tip2: Leave blank to use system-detected location

os.title: OS Variable
os.tip: OS info will be appended to the end of the system prompt

agentPrompts.searchDesc: Quickly find the template you need from the rich prompt library

general.desc: Assistants for daily conversation and general tasks
